skill.name: azure-cost
skill.description: Unified Azure cost management: query historical costs, forecast future spending, and optimize to reduce waste
skill.summary: Query Azure costs, forecast spending, and optimize resources using official Cost Management APIs
skill.author: Microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 888 pattern matches (risk score 100/100), but evaluation confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is documentation-based markdown files containing Azure Cost Management API usage examples. Backtick patterns are PowerShell command formatting, not shell execution. Path sequences are relative documentation links. URLs are legitimate Azure references. No malicious intent detected. Minor risk from documented CLI command examples that require Azure authentication.

medium_findings.title: External Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Documentation contains PowerShell and Azure CLI command examples for cost management operations. These are instructional examples, not executable code. Commands require explicit user authentication via az login and operate within Azure's permission model.

low_findings.title: External Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: References to Azure documentation and status pages (status.azure.com) are legitimate Microsoft domains used for error handling guidance.

content.user_title: Analyze and Optimize Azure Cloud Costs
content.value_statement: Organizations struggle to understand and control their Azure spending. This skill provides unified cost visibility, accurate forecasting, and actionable optimization recommendations using official Azure Cost Management APIs.
actual_capabilities: Query historical Azure costs by subscription, resource group, or billing account
Forecast future Azure spending with daily or monthly granularity
Identify cost optimization opportunities including orphaned and underutilized resources
Generate cost breakdown reports by service, resource, location, or tags
Analyze AKS cluster costs and Redis cache utilization
Validate pricing against official Azure pricing pages
limitations: Requires Cost Management Reader role or higher on target Azure scopes
Cannot deploy, provision, or modify Azure resources
Forecast accuracy depends on having at least 28 days of historical cost data
Does not provide security audits or diagnostic capabilities

use_cases.title: Monthly Cost Review
use_cases.description: Finance teams can query monthly cost breakdowns, identify top spending services, and generate reports for budget reconciliation and chargeback.

use_cases.title: Cost Optimization Initiative
use_cases.description: DevOps engineers can identify underutilized resources, right-size VMs, find orphaned resources, and implement cost-saving recommendations.

use_cases.title: Budget Planning
use_cases.description: Project managers can forecast end-of-month costs, project quarterly spending, and validate budget allocations against actual consumption trends.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cost Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Azure costs for the last 30 days broken down by service
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of current spending patterns

prompt_templates.title: Resource Group Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze costs for resource group 'production-westus' and show the top 10 most expensive resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Identifying cost drivers in a specific environment

prompt_templates.title: Cost Forecast
prompt_templates.prompt: Forecast my Azure spending for the rest of this month and compare it to our budget

prompt_templates.title: Optimization Recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Find cost optimization opportunities in my subscription including orphaned resources, underutilized VMs, and potential savings from rightsizing

output_examples.input: Show my Azure costs for last month
output: Top Services: Virtual Machines ($5,200), Azure SQL Database ($2,100), Kubernetes Service ($1,800)
Cost Trend: 8% increase from previous month
Recommendations: 3 underutilized VMs identified, potential savings of $450/month
Confidence: 85% based on 90 days of historical data
Key Drivers: Expected VM scale-up in May, new Redis cache deployment

best_practices: Always query actual costs from Azure Cost Management API before making optimization decisions
Include the total bill alongside any optimization recommendations for context
Validate pricing from official Azure pricing pages and account for free tiers and reservations
anti_patterns: Assuming costs without querying actual data from the Cost Management API
Using incorrect date ranges - use 30 days for costs, 14 days for utilization metrics
Executing destructive operations like resource deletion without explicit user approval and dry-run validation

faq.question: What Azure permissions do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Cost Management Reader role at minimum. For optimization recommendations that query resource utilization, add Monitoring Reader and Reader roles on the target scope.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy or modify Azure resources?
faq.answer: No. This skill is read-only for cost analysis. It can query costs, forecast spending, and recommend optimizations, but cannot deploy, provision, or modify any Azure resources.

faq.question: How accurate are the cost forecasts?
faq.answer: Forecast accuracy depends on historical data availability. The API requires at least 28 days of cost history. Accuracy improves with more data and stable consumption patterns.

faq.question: Can I query costs for multiple subscriptions at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Use a management group or billing account scope to query costs across multiple subscriptions. The skill supports subscription, resource group, management group, and billing account scopes.

faq.question: Why are my forecast results empty or showing errors?
faq.answer: Empty forecasts usually mean insufficient historical data (<28 days) or dates entirely in the past. Ensure your time period includes future dates and your scope has cost history.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Azure reservations and savings plans?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can query costs using ActualCost or AmortizedCost. AmortizedCost shows the impact of reservations and savings plans spread over their term.
